title: Fee Mismatch Adjustment Workflow
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: fee mismatch adjustment workflow.
assumptions: Each reconciliation_case (T1) represents a single fee mismatch instance; related system records (fee_code) are linked explicitly and exclusively when claimed.
Supporting_reference_files and authorization_artifact_tokens are single-purpose artifacts and are consumed or explicitly detached when their role is complete.
GL account (T10) and posting_instruction (T3) validation are authoritative for ledger alignment; passing those validations is necessary before final adjustment.
Queues (T15/T16) are mutually exclusive assignment targets for case routing and reflect separation of duties between operations and compliance.
risks: Parameter naming ambiguity during implementation could conflate fee_code, fee_component and counterparty_account roles; adopt distinct parameter names that mirror these type_roles.
Insufficient segregation of duties: wrong queue or approver assignment may permit unauthorized adjustments—ensure assignment_queue enforcement and audit logs.
External GL validation failures or stale posting_instruction templates may block closure; maintain synchronized GL profiles and templates.
Missing or single-use authorization_artifact_tokens left uncleared could prevent automated case completion; implement lifecycle management for authorization artifacts.
